biography: Paul Shenar (February 12, 1936 – October 11, 1989) was an American actor. He is best known for his roles in Scarface (1982), Raw Deal (1986), and The Big Blue (1998). A veteran of the Broadway stage, he was one of the founding members of the American Conservatory Theater in 1965.  Shenar was diagnosed with HIV/AIDS in 1983, and died from the disease in 1989 at age 53.
name: Paul Shenar
